title: auth — product analytics
description: Product-analytics events from the auth surface. Fired alongside operational metrics but with different consent tiers + property shapes. No PII on any event; identity_fingerprint used as the correlation key.

events.name: User Logged In
events.description: Successful login. Essential-tier because sessions-per-day is a foundational retention metric.

events.name: MFA Verified
events.description: MFA challenge completed. Drives the MFA-adoption dashboard.

events.name: Refresh Rotated
events.description: Refresh token rotation. Drives session-length dashboards.

events.name: Refresh Reuse Detected
events.description: Essential-tier because breach reporting depends on the count regardless of consent state.

events.name: Cross App SSO Start
events.description: Grant issued — signals user intent to switch Applications.

events.name: Cross App SSO Complete
events.description: Grant exchanged. Funnel with Cross App SSO Start.

events.name: New Device Login
events.description: Login from a device fingerprint not seen in 30 days.

events.name: Password Reset Requested
events.description: Only fires when the email resolves to a real Identity — unknown-email requests are silent (enumeration safety).

events.name: Impersonation Started
events.description: Essential-tier because impersonation compliance reporting depends on the count.

notes: identity_fingerprint / impersonator_fingerprint / target_fingerprint are SHA-256 hashes of the corresponding IDs — stable across events but never reversible to the underlying ULID.
device_fingerprint_short is the first 8 characters of the DeviceFingerprinter output — distinguishes across a per-User session list without being unique enough to correlate across Users.

Consent-tier decisions: 'essential' for events product cannot ship without (login count, MFA adoption baseline, breach signals, impersonation audit). 'functional' for growth + adoption dashboards. Downstream consent-honoring drops functional events for users at essential-only.
